summary: Implemented server-side HTML rendering with Go templates for landing page and project swimlane page. Created page.go with RegisterPages, handleLanding, and handleProject handlers. Created three HTML templates (landing.html, swimlane.html, error.html) with proper html/template auto-escaping for XSS protection. Templates embed JSON data in script tags for client-side JS consumption.
keyDecisions: [2.3] Templates loaded from embedded filesystem via go:embed using ParseFS with named template definitions ({{define "landing.html"}})
[2.3] toJson template function added via template.FuncMap to serialize data as template.JS for safe embedding in script tags
[2.3] RegisterPages sets HTML template on Gin engine via r.SetHTMLTemplate() so all page routes share the same template set
[2.3] Error page rendered for non-existent projects with 404 status code and HTML content type (not JSON)
[2.3] Removed old placeholder.html template replaced by actual landing.html, swimlane.html, and error.html templates
[2.3] Created placeholder JS files (landing.js, swimlane.js, detail-panel.js, activity.js, dagre.min.js) for future JS implementation

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GET / renders web/templates/landing.html with project cards data
GET /projects/:id renders web/templates/swimlane.html with feature and task data
web/templates/landing.html contains: page skeleton, container for project cards, embedded CSS link, embedded JS
web/templates/swimlane.html contains: page skeleton, SVG container for DAG, detail panel placeholder, activity sidebar placeholder
404 page rendered for non-existent project IDs
Integration tests: landing page returns 200 with project names, project page returns 200 with feature data, invalid project returns 404

notes: 21 new page handler tests added. Total handler package tests: 66 (42 middleware + 3 API + 21 page). Handler coverage: 91.5%. All 128 tests across the full project pass.
